name: todo_list_tool
summary: Manage a persistent shared todo list. Items survive process restarts and are visible to every peer agent that points at the same data directory.
constraints: All non-list responses return the affected item (or summary) as pretty-printed JSON.
`claim` records which agent owns the item; `add_dependency` records that one item blocks another, and `update` to `Completed` is rejected while a blocker is unfinished.
IDs are returned by `create` and `list`; the model must use them verbatim — they are not human-readable slugs.
anti_patterns: To send a one-off message to a peer — use `send_message_tool`.
To spawn a sub-agent that handles the work — use `agent_tool`.
cautions: IMPORTANT: `update` to `Completed` fails when the item has unfinished blockers. Resolve the blocker first or remove the dependency.
IMPORTANT: `delete` is irreversible — the item is removed, including its history. Prefer `update` to mark it `Completed` if you want to keep an audit trail.
output: Plain text. `create`, `update`, `get`, `claim` return pretty-printed JSON of the item; `list` returns a JSON array; `delete` returns `Item <id> deleted`; `add_dependency` returns `Dependency added: <from> blocks <to>`.
On failure: an error message — item not found, item already completed, blocked by an unfinished item, or unknown `action`.

action.description: Which subcommand to run: `create` adds a new item; `update` mutates an item's status or subject; `list` returns every item; `get` fetches one by id; `delete` removes one; `claim` assigns ownership; `add_dependency` records that one item blocks another.

status.description: New status (used by `update`). Setting `Completed` fails if the item has unfinished blockers.
